text: The proper function of man is to live, not to exist. I shall not waste my days in trying to prolong them. I shall use my time.
author: Jack London

text: Death is but crossing the world, as friends do the seas; they live in one another still. For they must needs be present, that love and live in that which is omnipresent. In this divine glass, they see face to face; and their converse is free as well as pure. This is the comfort of friends, that though they may be said to die, yet their friendship and society are, in the best sense, ever present, because immortal.
author: William Penn

text: We are closer to God when we are asking questions than when we think we have the answers.
author: Abraham Joshua Heschel

text: Time is free, but it's priceless. You can't own it, but you can use it. You can't keep it, but you can spend it. Once you've lost it you can never get it back.
author: Harvey MacKay

text: 'A friend loves at all times, and a brother is born for adversity.'
author: King Solomon, Proverbs 17:17

text: Have I not commanded you' Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged, for the Lord your God will be with you wherever you go.
author: Joshua 1:9

text: A wave can kill you. Or you can ride it. It's sometimes more dangerous to shy away. You can't live your life in fear. You have to be prepared to get on your board and stand on your feet.
author: Matt Haig

text: He who fears he shall suffer, already suffers what he fears.
author: Michel de Montaigne

text: Then He arose and rebuked the wind and said to the sea, 'Peace! Be still!' And the wind ceased and there was great calm.
author: Mark 4:39

text: I think we should follow a simple rule: if we can take the worst, take the risk.
author: Dr. Joyce Brothers

text: Whatever your hand finds to do, do it with all your might.
author: Ecclesiastes 9:10

text:
But those who trust in the Lord will find new
strength.
They will soar high on wings like eagles.
They will run and not grow weary.
They will walk and not faint.
author: Isaiah 40:31

text:
The time will come
when, with elation,
you will greet yourself arriving
at your own door, in your own mirror
and each will smile at the other's welcome,

and say, sit here. Eat.
You will love again the stranger who was your self.
Give wine. Give bread, Give back your heart
to itself, to the stranger who has loved you

all your life, whom you ignored
for another, who knows you by heart.
Take down the love letters from the bookshelf

the photographs, the desperate notes,
peel your own image from the mirror.
Sit. Feast on your life.
author: Derek Walcott

text:
Fix your mind upon its object. 
Hold to this, unswerving,
Disowning fear and hope,
Advance only upon this goal.
author: Arjun?s divine instruction

text:
Presence of mind, and courage in distress, 
Are more than armies to procure success.
author: John Dryden

text:
you can't make homes out of human beings' someone should have already told you that' 
and if he wants to leave'
then let him leave
'you are terrifying'
and strange and beautiful'
something not everyone knows how to love.
author: Warsan Shire
source: For Women Who Are Difficult to Love

text: Ah, but a man's reach should exceed his grasp, or what's a heaven for'
author: Robert Browning

text: No matter how love-sick a woman is, she shouldn't take the first pill that comes along.
author: Dr. Joyce Brothers

text: Whoever restrains his words has knowledge, and he who has a cool spirit is a man of understanding.
author: King Solomon, Proverbs 17:27

text: Listening, not imitation, may be the sincerest form of flattery.
author: Dr. Joyce Brothers

text: Knowledge is finite. Wonder is infinite.
author: Matt Haig

text: Yea, and if some god shall wreck me in the wine-dark deep, even so I will endure... For already have I suffered full much, and much have I toiled in perils of waves and war. Let this be added to the tale of those.
author: Homer

text: The world is too much with us.
author: William Wordsworth
source: Sonnet: The World is too much with us

text: Don't aim for perfection. Evolution, and life, only happen through mistakes.
author: Matt Haig

text: For even the Son of Man came not to be served but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.
author: Mark 10:45

text: For the ways are many, but death is one. Wherefore then should you be afraid'
author: Not sure

text: It's not 'how do I feel about them'' it's 'how do they make me feel''
author: Matthew Hussey

text:
The more you give, the more you get.
The more you laugh, the less you fret.
The more you do unselfishly, The more you live abundantly.
The more of everything you share, The more you'll always have to spare.
The more you love, the more you'll find 
That life is good and friends are kind.
For only what we give away, 
Enriches us from day to day.
author: Helen Steiner Rice

text: There is no happiness like that of being loved by your fellow creatures, and feeling that your presence is an addition to their comfort.
author: Charlotte Bront

text: Time heals everything. I don't need anything. Hallelujah.
author: Beyonc

text: Faith is the assurance of things hoped for, the conviction of things not seen. -Hebrews II
author: Bible
source: Paul's second letter to the Hebrews

text: When I look at myself in the mirror, I thank God the same old jerk is still staring back at me.
author: Roberto Canessa

text: 'There are years that ask questions, and years that answer.'
author: Zora Neale Hurston

text: An envious heart makes a treacherous ear.
author: Zora Neale Hurston

text: I'll tell you this: The braver I am, the luckier I get.
author: Glennon Doyle

text: Whimsy goes with everything.
author: Kirk Gleason

text: I was in my early thirties when I began, literally, to wake up to questions about my vocation. By all appearances things were going well, but the soul doesn't put much stock in appearances
author: Parker Palmer

text:
I have plowed my life this way 
Turned over a whole history
Looking for the roots of what went wrong
'.Enough
'I plowed to unearth last year's reasons - The farmer plows to plant a greening season
author: Parker Palmer

text: If equal affection cannot be, let the more loving one be me.
author: Dolly Alderton

text: The best things that I have experienced in life, I have had to wait for, and not comfortably.
author: Unknown

text: Let it be said that I am right rather than consistent.
author: Justice Marshall Harlan

text: Mood follows action. Just do the thing.
author: Shalane Flanagan
source: We Can Do Hard Things Podcast

text: Love aint somethin' lak uh grindstone dat's de same thing everywhere and do de same thing tuh everything it touch. Love is lak de sea. It's uh movin' thing, but still and all, it takes its shape from de shore it meets, and it's different with every shore.
author: Zora Neale Hurston

text: Most people are only as needy as their unmet needs. When their emotional needs are met, and the earlier the better, they usually turn their attention outward.
author: Amir Levine & Rachel Heller

text: Breathe out, then in. Find the self, then lose it once again.
author: Darrow

text: Our idea of God tells us more about ourselves than about Him.
author: Thomas Merton

text: You create your own luck.
author: Unknown

text: Fake it till you make it.
author: Unknown

text: Life begins outside of your comfort zone.
author: Unknown

text: Listen with the same passion of wanting to be heard.
author: Unknown

text: Pay attention to who you are with when you feel your best.
author: Unknown

text: Choose discomfort over resentment.
author: Unknown

text: Don't compare your insides with someone else's outside.
author: Unknown

text: Handsome is as handsome does.
author: Unknown

text: Love the thing that loves you back.
author: Unknown

text: Comparison is the thief of joy.
author: Unknown

text: But sometimes I think we choose our illnesses, because they benefit us in some way.
author: Unknown

text: Live a life of oh Wells instead of what if's
author: Unknown

text: If we only wanted to be happy, it would be easy; but we want to be happier than other people, which is almost always difficult, since we think them happier than they are.
author: Charles de Montesquieu

text: Everything you see or hear or experience in any way at all is specific to you. You create a universe by perceiving it, so everything in the universe you perceive is specific to you.
author: Unknown

text: Respect yourself enough to walk away from anything that no longer serves you, grows you, or makes you happy.
author: Robert Tew

text: It does not do to dwell on dreams and forget to live.
author: Dumbledor

text: People like to feel important. Make them feel important and they'll appreciate you.
author: Unknown

text: A healthy man wants a thousand things a sick man only wants one.
author: Confucius

text: Life is what happens when you're busy making plans.
author: Unknown

text: The only way out is through.
author: Unknown

text: The popular belief isn't always the correct one.
author: Unknown

text: You're your problem, but you're also your solution.
author: Unknown

text: The worst decision is indecision.
author: Unknown

text: I fear not the man who has practiced 10,000 kicks once, but I fear the man who has practiced one kick 10,000 times.
author: Bruce Lee

text: Hard work will open every door infront of you. But you will not be in front of every door.
author: Unknown

text: Go further, go farther, if not why bother'
author: Jay Z

text: Doubt kills more dreams than failure ever will.
author: Unknown

text: Everyone has a plan until they get punched in the face.
author: Unknown

text: You teach people how to treat you
author: Unknown

text: You cannot consider the measure of something without considering the measurer first.
author: Unknown

text: Face what you're afraid of and you'll be free.
author: Jordan Peterson

text: Bravery is better than safety.
author: Jordan Peterson

text: If you are surrounded by people who are dull, try listening more.
author: Jordan Peterson

text: Things you regret the most are things you value the most.
author: Unknown

text: When in doubt always reach out.
author: Unknown

text: The way we describe our experience becomes the memory.
author: Unknown

text: The harder you work the luckier you get.
author: Unknown

text: A bend in the road is not the end of the road.
author: Unknown

text: You can walk through life much easier with a smile on your face.
author: Unknown

text: Luck is the residue of design.
author: Branch Ricky

text: O God and Heavenly Father, Grant to us the serenity of mind to accept that which cannot be changed; courage to change that which can be changed, and wisdom to know the one from the other, through Jesus Christ our Lord, Amen.
author: Serenity Prayer

text: All we have to decide is what to do with the time that is given us.
author: Gandalf

text: Luxuries can often times become necessities.
author: Unknown

text: End' No, the journey doesn't end here. Death is just another path, one that we all must take. The grey rain-curtain of this world rolls back, and all turns to silver glass, and then you see it.
author: Gandalf

text: It is difficult to get a man to understand something when his salary depends on his not understanding it.
author: Upton Sinclair

text: A good leader takes a little more than his share of the blame, a little less than his share of the credit.
author: Unknown

text: You are not a failure until you start blaming others for your mistakes.
author: John Wooden

text: Biology enables, culture forbids.
author: Unknown

text: Always maintain a healthy skepticism for anyone claiming to be authority, at least till they prove themselves capable.
author: Unknown

text: But in the end, it's only a passing thing, this shadow. Even darkness must pass. A new day will come. And when the sun shines it will shine out the clearer.
author: Samwise Gamgee

text: The key to a successful leader is to earn respect for character, not rank.
author: Dick Winters

text: Do you want to walk around all day knowing that you could but you didn't'
author: David Goggins

text: There's only one standard. Your own.
author: Unknown

text: There's a difference between fear and losing your nerve.
author: Unknown

text: I know that I don't know.
author: Socrates

text: A spouse is not a destination but a fellow traveler.
author: Unknown

text: Luck is when preparation and opportunity meet.
author: Unknown

text: The devil you know is better than the devil you don't.
author: Unknown

text: Everybody is a hero in their own story.
author: Unknown

text: I try very hard not to take things personal that I don't think are meant to be personal.
author: Tim Cook

text: If you can't make a decision, go with your first choice. If you still don't know, the answer is no.
author: RJS

text: It is our choices...that show what we truly are, far more than our abilities.
author: Dumbledore

text: Without rain nothing grows, learn to embrace the storms in your life.
author: Unknown

text: Be loyal to what matters.
author: Arthur Morgan

text: Work like you're broke.
author: Unknown

text: Work while others are wishing.
author: Unknown

text: Nothing ventured nothing gained.
author: Unknown

text: May you live in interesting times.
author: Unknown

text: Conserve your forces and energies by keeping them concentrated at their strongest point. You gain more by finding a rich mine and mining it deeper, than by flitting from one shallow mine to another ' intensity defeats extensity every time.
author: Unknown

text: If you play silly games you will win silly prizes.
author: Unknown

text: Silence breeds whispers.
author: Unknown

text: In business output matters more than input.
author: Unknown

text: Take the word fair out of your vocabulary. Then you will be free.
author: Unknown

text: It is possible to commit no mistakes and still lose. That is not a weakness. That is life.
author: Unknown

text: Opportunity is not a lengthy visitor.
author: Stephen Sondheim

text: If I'm not for myself, who will be' If I'm only for myself, what am I'
author: Unknown

text: Knowledge isn't power until it is applied.
author: Unknown

text: The opposite of love is not hate, it's indifference.
author: Elie Wiesel

text: The very wind that seeks to blow out a fire may cause it to spread.
author: Unknown

text: It's hard to hate up close.
author: Michelle Obama

text: You can lead a horse to water but you can't make it drink.
author: Unknown

text: Wise men make more opportunities than they find.
author: Unknown

text: Happiness is a choice that requires effort at times.
author: Aeschylus

text: Don't fear nothin but don't be no fool.
author: Unknown

text: The learn-it-all will always do better than the know-it all.
author: Unknown

text: We judge others by their actions, we judge ourselves by our intentions.
author: Unknown

text: Hero worship might make Americans forget that laws mattered more than men.
author: Calvin Coolidge

text: Winning is easy. It's the struggle that defines the man.
author: Ron Howard

text: Words are wind.
author: George R.R. Martin

text: Do not wait for chance.
author: Unknown

text: Lust looks like love until it's time to sacrifice.
author: Unknown

text: Fools talk, cowards are silent, wise men listen.
author: Unknown

text: It is our choices that show who we are far greater than our ability.
author: Unknown

text: First thing you need to ask yourself about criticism is 'is it fair''
author: Dr J

text: Begin with the end in mind.
author: Unknown

text: What would you attempt to do if you knew you could not fail'
author: Unknown

text: Let your energy impact the people you're talking to, as opposed to the other way around.
author: Unknown

text: Battles are won months before they're fought.
author: Unknown

text: Plan like a pessimist and dream like an optimist.
author: Unknown

text: Nothing in the world can take the place of persistence. Talent will not; nothing is more common than unsuccessful men with talent. Genius will not; unrewarded genius is almost a proverb. Education will not; the world is full of educated derelicts.
author: Calvin Coolidge

text: What would you attempt to do if you knew you could not fail'
author: Unknown

text: Nobody knows what they're doing before they do it'growth happens outside of your comfort zone.
author: Unknown

text: Creativity is an active process, not a passive one.
author: Unknown

text: Start with what you want to achieve, instead of limiting yourself to what's realistic or sustainable.
author: Unknown

text: Your new life will cost you your old one.
author: Unknown

text: Star in your role.
author: Unknown

text: There's no great loss without some gain.
author: Unknown

text: It's hard to grow if you don't challenge yourself.
author: Unknown

text: It takes all type of people to make the world go around.
author: Unknown

text: There's a lid for every pot.
author: Unknown

text: There's only one standard. Your own.
author: Unknown

text: Well done is better than well said.
author: Unknown

text: Don't run away from what you don't want; run toward what you do.
author: Unknown

text: When you're in the monkey house for too long you stop smelling the shit.
author: Unknown

text: Not every challenge is trauma.
author: Unknown

text: Sometimes life gives you what you need not what you want.
author: Unknown

text: I'm going to support you in your failure as much as your success.
author: Unknown

text: The stock market is a device for transferring money from the impatient to the patient.
author: Warren Buffet

text: To know thyself is the beginning of wisdom.
author: Socrates

text: Constant vigilance.
author: Unknown

text: Where you go I will go, and where you stay I will stay. Your people will be my people and your God my God. Where you die I will die, and there I will be buried. May the LORD deal with me, be it ever so severely, if even death separates you and me.
author: Unknown

text: Business is a matter of human service.
author: MS Hershey

text: Know your way out before you go in.
author: Unknown

text: Impatients kills progress.
author: Ken Burns

text: Real Gs move in silence like lasagna.
author: Unknown

text: It is not the answers you bring back that matter, it is the person that you become while seeking them.
author: Unknown

text: Thriving is surviving.
author: Unknown

text: I can never be all the people I want and live all the lives I want. I can never train myself in all the skills I want. And why do I want' I want to live and feel all the shades, tones and variations of mental and physical experience possible in my life.
author: Sylvia Plath

text: You only live once, but if you do it right, once is enough.
author: Unknown

text: You have to laugh so you don't weep.
author: Unknown

text: It's true, of course, that, in the long run, the scoreboard for investment decisions is market price. But prices will be determined by future earnings. In investing, just as in baseball, to put runs on the scoreboard one must watch the playing field, not the scoreboard.
author: Warren Buffet

text: Great minds discuss ideas; average minds discuss events; small minds discuss people.
author: Eleanor Roosevelt

text: I consider my ability to arouse enthusiasm among my people the greatest asset I possess, and the way to develop the best that is in a person is by appreciation and encouragement.
author: Charles Schwab

text: He that has once done you a Kindness will be more ready to do you another, than he whom you yourself have obliged.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: I shall pass this way but once; any good that I can do or any kindness I can show to any human being; let me do it now. Let me not defer nor neglect it, for I shall not pass this way again.
author: Etienne de Grellet

text: Everyone likes a compliment.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: You can make more friends in two months by becoming interested in other people than you can in two years by trying to get other people interested in you.
author: Dale Carnegie

text: The expression one wears on one's face is far more important than the clothes one wears on one's back.
author: Dale Carnegie

text: Belief is not a matter of choice, but conviction.
author: Unknown

text: A plan is only as good as those who see it through.
author: Unknown

text: The best confidence builder is experience.
author: Unknown

text: Ignore your instincts at your peril.
author: Unknown

text: Fail with honor rather than succeed by fraud.
author: Unknown

text: It is a rough road that leads to the heights of greatness.
author: Unknown

text: Sometimes, accepting help is harder than offering it.
author: Unknown

text: Who's the more foolish, the fool or the fool who follows him'
author: Unknown

text: Humility is the only defense against humiliation.
author: Unknown

text: It's hard to be hungry when you have.
author: Michael Jordan

text: You can catch more flies with a drop of honey than a bucket of gall.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: I have no right to say or do anything that diminishes a man in his own eyes. What matters is not what I think of him, but what he thinks of himself. Hurting a man in his dignity is a crime.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupry

text: Man is condemned to be free; because once thrown into the world, he is responsible for everything he does. It is up to you to give [life] a meaning.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

text: Never doubt that a small group of thoughtful, committed citizens can change the world; indeed, it's the only thing that ever has.
author: Margaret Mead

text: May the worst days of your future be better than the best days of your past.
author: David Leadbeater

text: The optimist invents the aeroplane, the pessimist the parachute.
author: George Bernard

text: It is difficult to get a man to understand something, when his salary depends on his not understanding it.
author: Upton Sinclair

text: We are on earth to take care of life. We are on earth to take care of each other.
author: Unknown
